hero.tagline: The SSH terminal that feels like home on macOS.
hero.sub: Native Swift. Ghostty-powered. iCloud-synced. Open source.
hero.requirements: macOS 14 Sonoma or later · Apple Silicon & Intel

features.sectionSub: Every detail tuned to the platform. No Electron, no compromises.

swift.title: Native Swift
swift.body: AppKit, Metal, Core Text. Launch instantly, scroll at 120 Hz.

ghostty.title: Powered by Ghostty
ghostty.body: The fastest terminal renderer on macOS, embedded as libghostty.

icloud.title: iCloud sync
icloud.body: Hosts, credentials, settings, and snippets sync via CloudKit. Zero config.

sftp.title: SFTP transfers
sftp.body: Drag, drop, push, pull. A real file drawer next to your shell.

bookmarks.title: Remote bookmarks
bookmarks.body: Pin remote directories. One click to jump back where you left off.

snippets.title: Command snippets
snippets.body: A palette of your reusable commands. Synced. Searchable. Yours.

sync.title: Your terminal, on every Mac you own.
sync.body: Caterm uses CloudKit to sync hosts, credentials, settings, snippets, and themes between your Macs. Encrypted by Apple, scoped to your iCloud account. Nothing to set up, no account to create.
bullets: Hosts and credentials, end-to-end via Keychain + iCloud
Command snippets palette, instantly available everywhere

sftp.title: Files where your shell already is.
sftp.body: Pop the file drawer beside any session to browse, upload, and download. Pin the remote paths you keep returning to — they live in the bookmark popover, one keystroke away.

openSource.title: Made in the open.
openSource.body: Caterm is open source under the MIT license. The whole macOS app, including the libghostty integration and CloudKit sync layer, is in one Swift Package. Fork it, build it, ship it.
